meaning: v.讨价过高,使过量装填过度充电n.超载,过重的负担,过度充电
examples: Make sure they don't overcharge you for the drinks.
It prompted employees to overcharge for work and to complete unnecessary repairs on a companywide basis.
The host grabbed the bills and initiated a verbal assault upon the cabby, calling him a worthless parasite and a disgrace to their country for trying to overcharge visitors.
Some say profitable hospitals may be using local monopoly to overcharge insurers and patients.
If she files charges against Zimmerman, it would be wise not to "overcharge" the case.
